behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: A pure unit test of the parser can miss resolver wiring issues (e.g., lockfile traversal, workspace handling, deduping). This needs multiple modules (manifest reader + dependency graph resolver) with real fixture files to catch real misses without requiring external network.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner dependency-discovery against fixture projects (`package.json`+lockfile, `requirements.txt`+compiled lock) on disk; assert output dependency set includes all expected direct/transitive packages and excludes none. Validate against known golden dependency graphs.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Mocked API clients can hide real request-shape/version-range bugs (query params, range encoding, server-side interpretation). To catch true failures, exercise real HTTP behavior against the actual vulnerability API.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner end-to-end query path against the real vulnerability database API using controlled dependencies/versions; assert returned CVEs differ when only version range changes, and verify no CVE is reported for unaffected ranges of the same package name.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic ordering logic with no I/O boundary. In-process tests over representative CVE lists catch comparator mistakes directly.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test comparator/sort function with mixed severities and equal-severity CVEs with different CVSS values; assert exact order and stable tie behavior.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Mocking serialization can miss real parser/writer side effects (format churn, reordered keys, incidental edits). Need real manifest read-modify-write across modules and filesystem fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Apply patching flow to real manifest fixtures on disk; diff original vs updated file and assert exactly one dependency version field changed, with byte-for-byte equality for all unrelated entries/sections.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is external Git hosting API/repo state (branch creation, commit, PR diff correctness). In-process mocks can falsely pass while real auth/permissions/diff behavior fails.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner against a real test repository and token; verify branch and PR are created, PR diff contains only one dependency version bump, and no unrelated file changes appear.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump classification is deterministic, local logic. No external dependency is required to catch incorrect comparisons.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test semver decision function with representative cases (`1.x->2.x`, pre-1.0 nuances if supported, metadata/prerelease variants) and assert breaking-change flag correctness.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This validates cross-module control flow (semver classifier -> decision engine -> PR client/report output). Unit tests on a single function can miss wiring errors; no real external API is strictly required if PR client is replaced by a spy adapter.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner pipeline with fixture indicating a major bump and injected PR adapter spy; assert no PR-create call is made and output/report includes explicit review-required flag with dependency context.
